reasoning: A major conflict resulting in 100+ uniformed casualties is extremely rare. Even the severe May 2025 crisis referenced in the background ("worst fighting in decades") did not clearly exceed 100 uniformed casualties, with reported military casualties being much lower (e.g., 11 troops dead on the Pakistani side). Three out of the four months in the forecasting window (June, July, August) have passed peacefully, and searches confirm the ceasefire established in mid-May has continued to hold without any major disruptions through late August 2025. With only 30 days remaining in the window, no recent precipitating events, and a crowd forecast of 1%, the likelihood of a massive spike in conflict reaching the 100 uniformed casualty threshold by September 30 is negligible. I estimate this at the lower bound of 2%.
